reviews.review_description: it's a quick game that you can play it's very fun and i really like it because you can play with your friends and stuff and they have training for new players and you can change your name anytime in the game if you don't like it and you can customize your character i just like the people in the game you have a great community

Love this game dispite it’s flaws. There are a couple of things that is very annoying. 

The worst is the lagging where other players can be up to a second ahead if you in time and can shoot you after you take cover behind somerhing.

Also, almost every player has created new users and start over as ”rookies”, most level 30-40 players are actually level 150 and if you play against a 150, it's probably a 300+.

Third, when a player leave a game it can take up to 2-3 minutes before the avatar leave and during this time the team is missing a player. 

Four, there are a lot of bugs in the game, if you try to jump over a payload you will probably die 2/5 times, if you push up against a wall sometimes you will explode an if you in shootout jump directly down to the machine gun you will explode. 

Five, there isn't any way to create a game that is locked to levels above 40. This mean that once your above level 40 you always end up playing against level 300 and die all the time.

Six, how hard should it när be to make the games even, even though each team has different leveled players? Just add some frickin rules depending on the different level a player has. 

It feels like the developers only focus is on creating new guns and skins, instead of enhancing the player experience.
Game is interesting. Not my favorite game and haven’t played online but happy developers made a free game we can own and can play without the internet. Glad I bought their other games.
I used to play this game back then and, I loved it it had thriving player base, but now that I play there isn't much people on the playing the game.

The game is lowkenuenly peak but it does have one problem. When a team is winning round after round it should have a way to make both teams equal and when that happens disabled team switching so it won't be the way it was. Just one team winning all the time

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the five stars and for supporting us with those purchases! We're glad you're enjoying the multiplayer experience. We hear you on the bot ratio—it's something we're always working to balance as our player base grows. Your feedback helps us improve, so we really appreciate it. Keep grinding, and you'll be dominating those matches in no time!

Cheers, and have the BEST day!

- TF

reviews.review_title: Best game oculus has to offer
reviews.review_description: hyper Dash is in my opinion one of the best games you can play on an oculus. The game offers fast paced, shooting, accurate skill based matchmaking (based off levels) and so many unique, maps, skins, and game modes to offer. On top of that, they have a discord and a competitive league for those of you who like a more competitive gameplay. You can win money for clans and have the greatest time of your life. Definitely recommend.￼￼

Finally uninstalled after playing a string of games where I got insta killed dozens of times with headshot after headshot.  I am too old to be playing this game, but am a fairly decent player and used to enjoy it.  Too many players can hop across an entire map and get a perfect kill shot time after time.  They might actually be that good, but it seems suspicious, feels like you are shooting blanks against perfect headshots.  The game play is amazing and there are good games from time to time, but the players base is sooo limited, fair matches are hard to find.  The days of casual enjoyable game play seem to be over.
This game never bores me
Just the best shooter

Its A cool game but some players are way better then me and i feel irrelivant but its cool . You and the team can work together and learn how to cooperate you will crash out though and likely get rage baited

This once was my favorite Quest game. I would make time daily after work to play a few hours since it's so addictive. However, the game is now nigh unplayable due to the dwindling player base and advanced level "tryhards" who routinely dominate each and every public lobby they join. 

HyperDash is a bit of a hard sell because it's difficult to describe the gameplay. Think of it as a normal 5v5 shooter where the objective of each map differs depending on the map (some maps you have to take control of a centrally located "button" for 300 seconds, others are a free-for-all deathmatch, others you have to work together to slowly move a cart from one side of the map to another). I didn't think I'd like this game since "dashing" and "teleporting" seemed the same. However, this game actually incorporates both smooth locomotion as well as dashing seamlessly. 

HyperDash is legitimately some of the most fun I've had playing a video game since some members of the community are hilarious and a blast to hang with. An evenly balanced Payload or Domination match is a true sight to behold, especially with teammates who communicate and take the game at least somewhat seriously.

However, the main problem with this game lies in the player base...or lack thereof. Don't get me wrong, the game is far from dead as you can fairly easily find full servers of players ready to enjoy the game. But you see, the number of players has been dwindling for quite some time and, as a result, most of the remaining players are extremely high level and will routinely curbstomp any server they join. Remember the thing I said about evenly balanced matches? Well, those are a rarity as 95% of my games nowadays involves 1-3 clanmates destroying any server they join. 

Dash League has become the focus of this game and, as such, anyone who just wants to play for fun is no longer welcome. It's unfortunate, but I'm quitting this game for good as it's no longer fun for the average player.

reviews.review_title: Great game but networking is a concern
reviews.review_description:
Initial comment Sept 22:

The content, graphics and gameplay is fantastic - however there seems to be a lot of issues around networking which creates a very frustrating experience. 

They are migrating the network library so I hope to see some improvement in the future.

Update: 
So since this comment, the network library hasn't been delivered and a bunch of other random stuff nobody within the community suggested or asked for has been added. And a fair few of these have been detrimental to the gaming meta & community. It's really poor and too much, especially with such a clear channel liase with the community before making such changes.
Love the different maps, and weapons selection... Overall amazing time playing the game

The best and funny

Hooked. As soon as you start playing, you get hooked. With various game modes, a multitude of maps and a full arsenal of weapons, this is definitely one of the best VR FPS shooters out there. Hyper Dash brings the teleportation movement and joystick movement combo to a whole new level. I honestly have nothing bad to say about this game.

Amazing job to Triangle Factory! Thank you for such an outstanding experience.

I'm gonna try and adress what people mark this game down on.
1. High levels joining lower lobbies, if you are under level 40 there are dedicated servers for you. 
2. Post dash (getting hit where you werent) as far as I know, this is client side and can't be helped it's down to ping and packet loss.
3. More guns, great, but maybe for a 2nd game mode, I Luke the mix of guns and def down to personal opinion. (Same with melee)
I like how replayable it is and the beginner pro scene and league scene. I've had it for a few years and I never once got bored but I do hate people who only use smg or shotguns.
